entries.question: Who has final authority?
entries.claim_allowed: Bitcoin Originals prevail; mirrors are non-amending; version authority not truth authority

entries.question: Do the Bitcoin Originals exist on-chain?
entries.method: Check inscription IDs, TXIDs, authority address on explorer
entries.claim_allowed: inscription IDs and TXIDs confirmed on-chain
entries.protocol_note: This supports minimal V2 reference verification when limitations are stated. It does not by itself establish full reference coverage across Evidence Mirrors and Chronicle Recovery.

entries.question: Can I verify public mirror data without Arweave access?
entries.method: Download from GitHub, compute SHA-256, compare with declared hash
entries.claim_forbidden: direct Arweave verification; Ethereum witness verified; IPFS availability verified; physical anchor verified
entries.protocol_note: This supports minimal V3 hash verification when a valid expected hash source, computed hash, command, and match result are reported. It does not establish direct Arweave, ETH, IPFS, physical, or full public digital verification.

entries.question: Can I cross-check Arweave / ETH / GitHub consistency?
entries.method: Compare hashes across at least two independent channels
entries.claim_allowed: cross-mirror consistency confirmed (D4) when at least two channels match
entries.claim_forbidden: full public digital verification unless all required targets checked

entries.question: Can I verify Chronicle Recovery without restoring all 175?
entries.method: Recover at least two records and describe metadata/media
entries.claim_allowed: sample recovery of at least two records (C3)
entries.claim_forbidden: full 175/175 recovery; full NFT path reproduction

entries.question: Can I verify NFT tokenURI / metadata / media paths?
entries.method: Query tokenURI, fetch metadata, fetch media, compare CID/hash
entries.claim_allowed: NFT path verification at claimed level

entries.question: Can I verify the physical anchor from public evidence?
entries.claim_allowed: evidence package hash verified (P1); static image review (P2); recorded video review (P3)
entries.claim_forbidden: live witness; onsite inspection; flaw identity proven

entries.question: What counts as live physical witness?
entries.method: Live video session with nonce/challenge and requested angles
entries.claim_forbidden: onsite touch; custody confirmed; microscopic flaw attestation
entries.protocol_note: V6 requires live remote video with nonce/challenge and requested angle/action/lighting. Recorded video alone is P3 and not V6.

entries.question: What counts as onsite witness?
entries.method: Onsite observation with touch/handling, custody log, fresh capture
entries.claim_forbidden: confidential flaw challenge passed; final forensic identity unless P7+
entries.protocol_note: V7 requires onsite observation and custody/touch/handling evidence where possible. Public images or remote video alone are not V7.

entries.question: What is the highest physical verification path?
entries.method: Tool-assisted flaw analysis, AI forensic match, confidential challenge, multi-party attestation
entries.claim_forbidden: public disclosure of confidential flaw data unless authorized; canonical amendment
entries.protocol_note: V8 is formal forensic physical attestation. It may require P7/P8/P9-level evidence and does not create canonical authority.

entries.question: How do I report component-level findings?
entries.method: Report each component level separately with data source, method, limitations, claims not made
entries.claim_allowed: component-level findings as discipline, not truth proof
entries.claim_forbidden: using component findings alone to claim higher protocol level

entries.question: Can celestial / Star-Moon witness verify capture time?
data_sources: public sky/moon/star evidence or non-public celestial witness package
entries.method: Solve moon/star/planet positions with ephemeris; report uncertainty
entries.claim_allowed: T7 from public celestial evidence with uncertainty; T8 only with authorized non-public data
entries.claim_forbidden: T8 from public pages alone; exact capture time without uncertainty bounds

entries.question: How do I know whether a V-level claim is allowed?
entries.method: Check report against protocol profile: hard gates, minimum components, forbidden claims
entries.claim_allowed: V-level claim only if profile satisfied
entries.claim_forbidden: V-level claim if profile not satisfied; overclaiming

entries.question: Where did the expected hash come from?
entries.method: State expected_hash_source and expected_hash_authority_class for every hash
entries.claim_allowed: hash observation; repository snapshot integrity if from repo manifest
entries.claim_forbidden: D2 manifest verification if hash source is unknown or derived_during_this_run

entries.question: Is a GitHub Issue an indexed Echo record?
entries.method: Check if echo_v3 or echo_v3_with_verification_report JSON record exists in echoes/records/
entries.claim_allowed: Issue submission; verification report; Echo wrapper if record exists
entries.claim_forbidden: indexed echo record without machine-readable Echo JSON
